title: Live Project Index
tagline: Local DuckDB index of models, columns, tests, edges
summary: The Live Project Index is a local DuckDB index of your dbt project's metadata — every model, column, test, and DAG edge — that reflects the actual last-run state, including pass/fail test results, run results, and node timings. It knows grain, primary-key markers, schemas, and materializations out of the box, so dbt Wizard answers structural and state questions by querying the index rather than parsing files. The describe, search, and changeset tools all read from this index, attaching DAG context to every result and computing epoch-based diffs since the last flush.
whyDifferent: A general AI coding agent reconstructs project structure every turn by reading .sql and .yml files and grepping for string matches, which yields textual references with no notion of grain, PK markers, materialization, or whether a test currently passes. dbt Wizard instead queries a pre-built DuckDB index that already holds the resolved DAG, column catalog, and last-run state, so a search for a concept returns models with lineage, materialization, and live test status attached. Where grep returns "these files contain this string," the index returns "this model at this grain, with this PK, these failing tests, and these downstream consumers."

capabilities.title: Last-run state baked in
capabilities.detail: The index records the actual results of the most recent dbt run/test: per-test pass/fail, row counts, run results, and per-node timings. describe surfaces a model's current test status without re-running anything.

capabilities.title: Grain, PK, schema, materialization out of the box
capabilities.detail: Every node carries its grain, primary-key markers, target schema, and materialization (table/view/incremental/ephemeral). No file parsing or inference is needed to answer 'what is the grain of fct_orders.'

capabilities.title: Dual-mode search with DAG context
capabilities.detail: search runs either BM25 ranking over model/column metadata and descriptions, or regex against raw or compiled SQL. Either way, every hit comes back with its DAG position (upstream/downstream edges, layer) attached.

capabilities.title: Full node introspection
capabilities.detail: describe returns columns, compiled SQL, catalog data types, test results, and lineage for any node in a single structured call — the equivalent of opening the .sql, the schema.yml, the catalog.json, and run_results.json simultaneously.

capabilities.title: Epoch-based changeset diffs
capabilities.detail: changeset reports exactly which nodes, columns, and tests changed since the last index flush, with optional downstream impact, so the SE can see the delta without diffing files by hand.

capabilities.title: Structured tool calls, not grep
capabilities.detail: All three tools are MCP calls (mcp__dbt_index__*) backed by the local DuckDB index. Results are typed records, not raw text scrapes, so they are deterministic and machine-consumable for follow-on tools.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__describe
tools.purpose: Full node introspection from the index: columns and catalog data types, compiled SQL, current test results, lineage, grain, PK markers, schema, and materialization — all in one structured call, no file reads.

params.name: node
params.desc: The model/source/seed/snapshot node to introspect, e.g. 'fct_orders' or 'model.shop.int_orders_enriched'.

params.name: include_sql
params.desc: Include raw and compiled SQL for the node. Default true.

params.name: include_tests
params.desc: Include per-test pass/fail status from the last run. Default true.

params.name: include_lineage
params.desc: Include immediate upstream/downstream edges. Default true.

params.name: include_columns
params.desc: Include the column catalog with data types and PK markers. Default true.

tools.returns: A structured node record: grain, PK marker(s), schema, materialization, columns with types, compiled SQL, last-run test results (pass/fail + counts), and immediate lineage edges.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__search
tools.purpose: Find nodes and columns touching a concept. Runs either BM25 over metadata/descriptions or regex against raw/compiled SQL, returning every hit with its DAG context attached.

params.name: query
params.desc: Search term (BM25) or regex pattern (regex mode), e.g. 'refund' or 'sum\\(.*amount.*\\)'.

params.name: mode
params.desc: 'bm25' for metadata/description ranking, or 'regex' for pattern matching against SQL. Default 'bm25'.

params.name: target
params.desc: For regex mode: 'raw' or 'compiled' SQL. Default 'compiled'.

tools.returns: Ranked list of matching nodes/columns, each with relevance score (BM25) or match snippet (regex) plus DAG context: layer, materialization, immediate upstream/downstream edges.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__changeset
tools.purpose: Epoch-based diff of what changed in the index since the last flush — nodes, columns, and tests added/removed/modified — with optional downstream impact attached.

params.name: since
params.desc: Epoch/flush marker to diff against. Defaults to the last flush.

params.name: include_downstream
params.desc: Attach downstream nodes affected by each change. Default false.

params.name: node_filter
params.desc: Optional node or selector to scope the diff, e.g. 'stg_products' or 'tag:finance'.

tools.returns: A structured delta: changed nodes, added/dropped/retyped columns, test additions/removals and status flips since the epoch, optionally with downstream impact lists.

examplePrompts.prompt: Find every model that touches refunds and tell me the grain and current test status of the most relevant one.
examplePrompts.intent: Locate concept-related models, then introspect the top hit's grain and live test state.

examplePrompts.prompt: What's the grain and primary key of fct_orders, and are any of its tests currently failing?
examplePrompts.intent: Single-node introspection for grain, PK, and last-run test results.

examplePrompts.prompt: Which models reference the brand_name column in their compiled SQL?
examplePrompts.intent: Regex search against compiled SQL for a column usage, with DAG context.

examplePrompts.prompt: Show me everything that changed in the project since my last build, and what's downstream of it.
examplePrompts.intent: Epoch-based changeset diff with downstream impact.

examplePrompts.prompt: List the gold-layer models with descriptions mentioning customer lifetime value and show me the columns of the best match.
examplePrompts.intent: BM25 metadata search scoped to a layer, then column introspection.

examplePrompts.prompt: Did any tests flip from passing to failing since the last flush?
examplePrompts.intent: Changeset diff focused on test status flips.

examplePrompts.prompt: What materialization and target schema does int_orders_enriched use, and what columns does it expose?
examplePrompts.intent: Node introspection for materialization, schema, and column catalog.

interaction.scenario: An SE is exploring an unfamiliar e-commerce dbt project and wants to find all models touching the 'refund' concept, then inspect the grain, tests, and columns of the most relevant one — entirely from the index, with no file parsing.
interaction.userPrompt: Find all models that deal with refunds, then show me the grain, primary key, columns, and current test status of the most relevant one.

events.body: Find all models that deal with refunds, then show me the grain, primary key, columns, and current test status of the most relevant one.
I'll query the Live Project Index for nodes touching 'refund' using BM25 over metadata and descriptions, then introspect the top hit. No files are read — this comes from the local DuckDB index.
Three nodes touch refunds across bronze, silver, and gold. The top BM25 hit is fct_refunds (gold). I'll run full introspection on it for grain, PK, columns, and last-run test status.

The index shows fct_refunds passes its uniqueness and not-null checks on refund_id, but the relationships test to fct_orders.order_id is currently failing with 14 orphan refunds as of the last run — surfaced from stored run state, not a re-run.

Three models deal with refunds: stg_refunds (bronze, view) -> int_order_refunds (silver, ephemeral) -> fct_refunds (gold, table). The most relevant is fct_refunds.

Grain: one row per refund_id. Primary key: refund_id (passes unique + not_null).

Columns: refund_id (VARCHAR, PK), order_id (VARCHAR), customer_id (VARCHAR), refund_amount (NUMBER(18,2)), refund_reason (VARCHAR), refunded_at (TIMESTAMP_NTZ). Schema analytics_gold.

Test status from the last run (2026-05-29): unique and not_null on refund_id pass, but the relationships test from order_id to fct_orders.order_id is FAILING with 14 orphan rows. fct_refunds feeds fct_orders and dim_customers, so those 14 unmatched refunds are worth chasing before they propagate. Want me to trace those orphans against the warehouse?

seNotes: The selling point is state, not just structure: the index already holds the last run's pass/fail tests, row counts, and timings, so describe answers 'is this model healthy right now' without spending a single warehouse credit re-running anything.

Lead the demo with grain and PK. A general agent has to read schema.yml and infer; the index returns grain ('one row per X') and PK markers as first-class fields — that lands instantly with data engineers.

search has two modes worth showing back to back: BM25 over metadata for concept discovery ('find refunds'), then regex against compiled SQL for surgical column-usage hunts ('where is brand_name actually referenced'). Both attach DAG context to every hit.
changeset is epoch-based, diffing since the last index flush — frame it as 'what moved since my last build,' including test status flips, not a git diff of files.

Reinforce that none of this is file reading or grep: it's structured MCP calls (mcp__dbt_index__*) against a local DuckDB index, so results are typed, deterministic, and chainable into impact/lineage/warehouse follow-ups.
